task: I need to send a short update to a few people about a work issue that’s taking longer than expected, and I want the message to be easy to follow without adding extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft it so it sounds professional and fits a normal work update?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, professional version that stays factual and keeps the next step clear:

> Quick update: this issue is taking longer than expected, but it is being corrected. I’m working through the remaining pieces now and will send the next update by [day/time]. Thanks for your patience.

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly firmer version
- a more neutral Slack version
- an email version with a little more context

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close. I’d want it a little more specific and less soft — like, what’s delayed, what people should expect next, and no extra fluff.

Something like:

Quick update: this is taking longer than expected because I’m still working through the remaining pieces. I’ll send the next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll let you know.

If you want, I’d rather have 2-3 versions: one more direct, one for email, one for Slack.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 3 tighter versions you can use.

### 1) More direct
> Quick update: this is taking longer than expected because I’m still working through the remaining pieces. I’ll send the next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll let you know.

### 2) Email version
> Quick update on this issue: it’s taking longer than expected because I’m still working through the remaining pieces needed to finish the review. I’ll send the next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll let you know.

### 3) Slack version
> Quick update: this is still in progress and taking longer than expected while I work through the remaining pieces. Next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll post it here.

If you want, I can also make them:
- a little firmer
- a little more neutral
- or tailored for people who are already frustrated

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is close. The Slack one is probably the cleanest, but I’d want the “remaining pieces” part to say what kind of thing is delaying it — not the full story, just enough so it doesn’t sound vague.

Like if it’s waiting on review, approval, data, whatever. Also maybe one version that sounds a little more matter-of-fact, not apologetic.

So yeah — 2-3 versions is still the right ask: direct, email, Slack, but a bit more specific.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are tighter versions with a little more specificity, without getting wordy.

### 1) Direct
> Quick update: this is taking longer than expected because I’m still waiting on the remaining review/approval/data needed to finish it. I’ll send the next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll let you know.

### 2) Email
> Quick update on this issue: it’s taking longer than expected because I’m still waiting on the remaining review/approval/data needed to complete the work. I’ll send the next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll let you know.

### 3) Slack
> Quick update: this is still in progress and taking longer than expected while I’m waiting on the remaining review/approval/data needed to finish it. Next update by [day/time]. If anything changes before then, I’ll post it here.

### More matter-of-fact version
> Update: this is delayed while I’m waiting on the remaining review/approval/data needed to move it forward. I’ll send the next update by [day/time].

If you want, I can also make these:
- a little firmer
- a little more human
- or tailored for people who are already frustrated

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. The matter-of-fact one is probably the closest to what I wanted, and the Slack version is clean enough. No extra fluff, which is the main thing.
